mcqs.question: In the provided MPI code, what is the purpose of MPI_Comm_rank?

options.text: To get the unique identifier (rank) of the current process
To determine the total number of processes in the communicator
To initialize the MPI environment
To send a message to another process

mcqs.explanation: MPI_Comm_rank returns the rank (process ID) of the calling process within the specified communicator (MPI_COMM_WORLD). This allows each process to identify itself and execute different code paths based on its rank.

mcqs.question: What does MPI_Send's fifth parameter (tag) represent in point-to-point communication?

options.text: A user-defined message identifier to distinguish between different message types
The data type of the message being sent
The rank of the destination process
The size of the message buffer

mcqs.explanation: The tag parameter is an integer that identifies the message type. It allows the receiving process (using MPI_Recv) to selectively receive messages with specific tags, enabling more complex communication patterns.

mcqs.question: In the example code, why is the message buffer size specified as 50 in both MPI_Send and MPI_Recv?

options.text: It must match the declared size of the character array to prevent buffer overflow
It represents the actual length of the string being sent
It is the maximum number of processes allowed
It indicates the message priority level

mcqs.explanation: The count parameter (50) specifies the maximum number of elements to send/receive. Using the full buffer size ensures safe transmission even if the actual message is shorter, preventing buffer overflow issues.

mcqs.question: What is the role of MPI_COMM_WORLD in MPI function calls?

options.text: The default communicator containing all processes
A special tag for broadcast operations
The initialization routine for MPI
A constant representing message priority

mcqs.explanation: MPI_COMM_WORLD is the default communicator that includes all processes launched by the program. It provides a communication context and defines the group of processes that can communicate with each other.

mcqs.question: What happens if rank 1 executes before rank 0 in the example code?

options.text: MPI_Recv will block until rank 0's message arrives
The program will crash with a runtime error
MPI_Recv will return immediately with empty data
The processes will deadlock

mcqs.explanation: MPI_Recv is a blocking receive operation. If process 1 reaches MPI_Recv before process 0 sends the message, it will wait (block) until the matching message from process 0 arrives, ensuring proper synchronization.
